title: Amid a Slowing Economy, China Is Changing How It Judges Local Party Leaders
gist: The CCP's February-July 2026 'correct view of performance' (政绩观) campaign recalibrates how local party leaders are judged, moving away from GDP growth and infrastructure expansion toward political loyalty, social welfare, and long-term execution, an admission that the old growth-based legitimacy model no longer holds amid a slowdown. (Text is a truncated teaser ending in '[...]'.)

insights.claim: China's CCP launched a 'correct view of performance' (政绩观) campaign running February to July 2026 that explicitly acknowledges GDP growth and infrastructure expansion are no longer sufficient to sustain political legitimacy amid an economic slowdown, fiscal constraints, and rising societal expectations.
insights.speaker: Omkar Bhole
insights.so_what: A top-down signal that Beijing is downgrading the GDP-maximization incentive for local officials — bearish for the old debt-fueled local infrastructure growth engine.
insights.quote: the metrics that underpinned political legitimacy over the past four decades – particularly rapid GDP growth and infrastructural expansion – are no longer sufficient to sustain political legitimacy
insights.locator: 'Launched in February and expected to continue till July'

insights.claim: The CCP is redefining bureaucratic success from a narrow quantitative GDP measure toward a model combining political loyalty, social welfare, and long-term policy execution.
insights.speaker: Omkar Bhole
insights.so_what: Changes the incentive function for the officials who drive local spending and project approvals, with downstream effects on which sectors get prioritized.
insights.quote: shifting from a narrow, quantitative conception to a model that combines political loyalty, social welfare, and long-term policy execution
insights.locator: 'In response, the CCP is attempting to redefine'

insights.claim: The campaign explicitly frames data manipulation and wasteful prestige 'image projects' designed for immediate visible results as manifestations of a distorted performance view that undermines policy effectiveness and public trust.
insights.speaker: Omkar Bhole
insights.so_what: Official discouragement of prestige infrastructure and fudged statistics implies tighter scrutiny on local investment quality and reduced low-return capex.
insights.quote: Practices ranging from data manipulation to wasteful prestige infrastructure are now explicitly framed as manifestations of a distorted performance view
insights.locator: 'This reframing seeks to correct persistent distortions'
